folio.label: 47v
folio.l: ¢ Thus endetú the Millers tale , & bygynnetú the ¸loge of the Reve

note.notetxt: 1 The tale begins on f. 47v. I suspect the prologue was copied before the miller was finished on the recto. This is an additional bifolia inserted in the quire.ODR

folio.l: Wha~ne folk han laughen , at æis nyce cas
Of Absolou~ , and hende Nicholas
Dyuäse folk , diuersly they seyde
But for the more part , they loughe & pleyde
Ne at this tale , I saw noman hiö greue
But it were oonly , Oswold the Reue
By cause he was , of Carpenteres craft´
A litel Ire , is , in his hert laft´
He gan to grocche , and blamed it a lyte
So thike q¶ he , ful wel coude I the quyte
With bleryngö , of a proude Milleres eye
If æat me list , to speke of rybaudye
But ik am old , me list nat pleye for age
Gras tyme is dou~ , my foder is now forage
This white top~ , wrytetú myne olde ©eres
Myn hert is also , mouled , as myne heeres
But if I fare , as doth an open ars
That ilke fruyt , is euäe lengere the wers
Til it be roten , in mullok or in stree
We olde men , I drede , so fare we
Til we be roten , kan we nat be rype
We hope alwey , while æ the world wol pipe
For in oure wyl , there stekith euäe a nayl
To han an hore heer , and a grene tayl
As hath a leek , for though oure mygút be gon
Oure wyl , desireth folye , euäe in onö
For wha~ne we moun nat don , than wol we speke
ñet in oure asshen old , ys fire I reke
Foure gledes han we , which I shal deuyse
Auauntyngö , lyingö , Angyr , Coueytise
Theise foure sparcles , longen vn to elde
Oure olde lymes , moun wel ben vn welde
But wyl ne shal faylen , that is soth
And ©et haue ik alwey , a coltyssú toth
As many a ©ere , as it is passed henne
Sithe that my tappe , of lyf , bygan to renne
For sekirly , whan yk was born , a non
Detú drougú the tappe of lyf , and let it gonö
And euäe sithe , hath so , the tappe I ronne
Til that almost , al empty is the tonne
The streme of lyf , now droppeth on the chyme
The sely tonge , may wel rynge & chymbe
Of wrecchednesse , that passed is ful ©ore
With olde folk , saue , dotage is na more
¢ Whan æat oure host , had herd this sermonyngö
He gan to speke , as lordly as a kyngö
